artifacts.content:
Hi Elena,

Kids are finally down, and I’m catching up on today. I keep thinking about that Lincoln wellness committee meeting back on March 12, 2024 when you told me those moms were embarrassed to admit their Comcast got shut off in February over the $180 balance. That’s been stuck in my head every time I talk about “just go to the website” for the triennial results.

I ended up making sure the full report isn’t only living at https://www.maplegroveusd.org/departments/wellness/triennial-assessment. Families can quietly ask Student Services at 2145 Oak Ridge Drive, Suite 300 for a printed copy without having to say, in front of everyone, that their internet was cut. It feels like such a small thing, but I keep picturing how you said they looked when they talked about Comcast.

Related to Lincoln, remember how in May 2023 I shared that password‑protected Google Drive folder with Principal Olson so she could print a few copies of the draft triennial results (without the raw bullying and weight‑teasing comments) for Sofia’s 5th‑grade parents at the May 18 Lincoln Family Wellness Night? I’m glad we did that—those comments were rough, and I wouldn’t want the kids reading them in the hallway either.

On the home front: Sofia’s running club meets again Thursday, and Mateo has soccer after school if the fields dry out. I told them pizza Friday is still on, even if Mr. “Smart Snacks” here keeps pushing roasted chickpeas at work.

I’ll handle Wednesday’s late committee meeting; can you grab the kids and maybe just do rice, beans, and veggies? I’ll text when I’m leaving Suite 305.

Besos,
Miguel

Hi Karen,

Quick follow‑up on a couple of things while I’m still at the district office.

First, I checked the public wellness policy link again this morning — the one at https://www.maplegroveusd.org/board/policies/5500-wellness. The Board still hasn’t stripped out the March 15, 2023 complaint materials from when Laura Jensen filed that formal grievance about Aiden’s BMI screening in the Lincoln nurse’s office. His height, weight, and obesity risk classification are still sitting there in the appendix of the Policy 5500 PDF. I’ve got a note to call the Board secretary on 3/20/23 to get that confidential appendix removed from the public version.

On a related front, I’m also worried because the PDF that’s live is still the one bundled with the November 7, 2022 closed‑session minutes where Board Member Karen Olson read out my written reprimand for mishandling student allergy information on that Lincoln field trip. I’ve already emailed my union rep asking HR to separate those minutes from the public policy document.

This all feels extra ironic given how careful I’ve been lately—double‑checking Infinite Campus custody flags after that September 2023 complaint so our SchoolMessenger blasts don’t go to non‑custodial parents, and rerouting the triennial results link through the wellness subpage in January 2024 after the Lincoln parent in eating‑disorder recovery called about the BMI charts popping up on her daughter’s tablet. I even left that printed copy under her name at the Lincoln front desk for pickup on 1/22/24.

Anyway, I’ll keep you posted once I hear back from the Board office. Hope your day wasn’t completely swallowed by testing schedules.

Best,
Miguel

Personal planning note – access + outreach

1) Triennial results access
- Keep main posting at https://www.maplegroveusd.org/departments/wellness/triennial-assessment.
- Remember why we added alternative routes: Elena’s Lincoln moms whose Comcast got shut off in Feb 2024 over the $180 bill; they were embarrassed to say in the March 12, 2024 meeting that they didn’t have internet. Confidential printed copies via District Student Services, Suite 300, so people can just ask at the desk.
- Continue quiet drop‑offs at Rosa’s building in Brooklyn Center. Since June 2023, I’ve been bringing extra printed triennial reports during my weekly visits so the Somali grandmothers don’t have to call the wellness line at (763) 555‑0100 and have their grandkids translate the sections about obesity risk and mental health referrals.
- For undocumented parents in Mateo’s class: after Andrea Peterson mentioned on 10/10/23 that several families are afraid to give their home address, keep telling them they can read the report in the Lincoln main office instead of waiting for the spring postcard with wellness@maplegroveusd.org.

2) Future revisions
- Next cycle: consider a separate, plain‑language summary for elders at Rosa’s building with less clinical language around obesity/mental health.
- Add a content warning or alternative view for BMI charts, especially after the January 2024 complaint from the Lincoln parent in eating‑disorder recovery.

3) To‑do
- Draft new blurb for Back‑to‑School packet explaining these options in simple English, Spanish, Somali.
- Ask Jenna Lee and Marcus Johnson at the next committee meeting whether the current web layout feels respectful or overwhelming to students.

Hi Adri,

I’ve been thinking a lot about how much of my “other stakeholder” brain is actually just our family.

You’ve basically been my secret weapon since January 2024, sending those de‑identified North Pines Community Health Clinic notes about your pediatric asthma and Type 2 diabetes kids at Lincoln and Maple Grove Middle. They’re technically just trend summaries, but they’ve helped me tighten our goals around inhaler access and sugar‑sweetened beverages. Let me know if your supervisor ever raises an eyebrow.

And then there’s Sofia. Ever since Dr. Herrera at North Pines Pediatrics flagged her elevated BMI and prediabetes risk in March 2023, I can’t look at Lincoln’s lunch carb counts or their limited PE minutes the same way. I keep using her situation—anonymously, obviously—when I push for more PE time and lower‑sugar options in cafeterias.

I also still feel awful about that April 10, 2024 night at Mom’s in St. Paul, when you helped me proof the Spanish translation of the wellness policy. The draft credits on the PDF at https://www.maplegroveusd.org/board/policies/5500-wellness still list you as “Adriana Ramirez, RN, North Pines Community Health Clinic (on leave for postpartum depression).” You told me you didn’t want your leave or diagnosis public, and I let that slip through. Fixing that credit line is at the top of my list this week.

On a lighter note: the kids want to come down to St. Paul next Sunday to kick a ball around with Daniel and hear more of his Duluth clinic stories about overuse injuries and that 16‑year‑old cross‑country runner. Apparently Uncle D is cooler than Dad now.

Love you,
Miguel

metadata.subject: You, Sofia, and all my “other stakeholders”

artifacts.content:
Event: Wellness committee meeting – student voices, access, privacy
Date: Friday, March 15, 2024
Time: 4:00–5:30 p.m.
Location: District Office Board Room, 2145 Oak Ridge Drive, Maple Grove, MN 55369
Attendees: Miguel Ramirez; Dr. Karen Whitfield; Cynthia Harper; Jenna Lee (11th – MGHS); Marcus Johnson (10th – MGHS); Dana Cho; Anthony Velasquez; Jason O’Reilly; parent reps; food service; others.

Agenda notes:
- Open with quick data review from 2020–23 triennial assessment.
- Public involvement: share how August Back‑to‑School packets, SchoolMessenger, and school newsletters are working; mention 11/9/23 Lincoln public info session where we used Sara Thompson’s “high‑need” PTO subgroup (FRP + food shelf referral families) for targeted invites about school meals and weekend backpack programs.
- Student voices: Jenna Lee to talk about fall 2023 experience at MGHS cafeteria (crowding, anxiety, skipped lunches, counselor at North Pines CHC). IMPORTANT: In personal notes I can record that she cried and referenced her anxiety diagnosis, but in official minutes for the triennial appendix, anonymize as “student representative” and remove clinic name.
- Marcus Johnson to update on vending changes and why we left his dad’s March 2022 no‑trespass/harassment situation out of the June 28, 2023 public summary after Rachel Nguyen’s advice.
- Access/notification: recap September 2023 custody complaint at Lincoln (Infinite Campus flags; removed three non‑custodial contacts before resending triennial link on 9/18/23).

Personal reminder:
- Pick up Sofia from running club by 3:25, drop her and Mateo at Elena’s mom Rosa’s in Brooklyn Center, then head back to the Board Room.
- After meeting, walk indoor track at community center if energy allows.

Hi Laura,

I wanted to document why today’s wellness outreach is going out a bit later than planned and flag a couple of related access issues.

First, timing: I’m writing from the Maple Grove Hospital ER. Mateo had an asthma attack at Lincoln this afternoon (10/5/22) and we’ve been here about three hours. He’s stable now, but I wasn’t able to send the fall SchoolMessenger wellness notification with the triennial assessment link at 4:00 p.m. as scheduled. I’ll push it out tomorrow once we’re home and he’s settled. I’ve attached the nearly final message with the link to https://www.maplegroveusd.org/departments/wellness/triennial-assessment for your quick review.

Second, privacy/access clean‑up: this comes on the heels of that September 2023 Lincoln parent who called furious that the triennial link went to her ex‑husband in violation of their custody agreement. Since 9/18/23 I’ve been personally double‑checking Infinite Campus custody flags before each SchoolMessenger blast and removed three non‑custodial parents from the wellness list.

I’m also thinking about families who never see the email in the first place—Elena’s Lincoln moms whose Comcast was shut off in February 2024 over a $180 bill, undocumented parents in Mateo’s class who are afraid to give addresses, and grandparents like José Alvarez who called me on February 8, 2024 asking me to email Policy 5500 directly from mramirez@maplegroveusd.org to his Gmail so he could show it to his attorney about his grandson’s bullying/anxiety situation at Lincoln.

Once things calm down at home, I’d like to sit down with you and Rachel Nguyen to tighten our outreach language so it’s both safer and more inclusive.

Thanks for understanding,
Miguel

metadata.subject: Wellness notification delay + some access concerns
metadata.location: Maple Grove Hospital ER waiting room

artifacts.content:
Private reflection – stakeholders who are really just “my people”

Therapist – Since spring 2022, my Tuesday 5:30 p.m. sessions with Dr. Karen Holt at Riverside Behavioral Health Clinic in Minneapolis have doubled as a place to unpack wellness policy drafts. We keep circling back to how the burnout and panic attacks I had during the 2020–21 school year are pushing me to load the policy with staff wellness and SEL language. Need to remember: my anxiety is not the district’s strategic plan.

Family health – Sofia’s March 2023 appointment with Dr. Luis Herrera at North Pines Pediatrics (elevated BMI, prediabetes warning, A1C concerns tied to Lincoln lunch carb counts and limited PE minutes) sits in the back of my mind every time I look at menu spreadsheets. I keep telling myself not to turn her into a case study, even as I use “a student I know” in committee conversations.

Extended family – Daniel’s August 2023 clinical rotation at the College of St. Scholastica’s partner clinic in Duluth gave me that story about the 16‑year‑old cross‑country runner with stress fractures and suspected eating disorder. I’ve already used it—stripped of identifiers—to argue for stricter weigh‑in and conditioning guidelines in the Maple Grove High School athletics handbook.

Rosa’s stories from her Brooklyn Center apartment about the Lincoln 3rd‑grader who got a three‑day suspension in October 2023 for bringing his stepdad’s vape pen, plus Adriana’s North Pines asthma/Type 2 diabetes trend notes, keep reminding me that our “other stakeholders” are living, breathing kids and caregivers, not bullet points.

Note to self: when I write or speak, protect their details as fiercely as I wish the Board had protected mine back in that November 7, 2022 reprimand.
